activities_projects.title: Analysing the Preamble
activities_projects.objective: Understand the key ideas of the Preamble
steps: Read the Preamble of the Indian Constitution
List the values mentioned such as justice, liberty, equality and fraternity
Explain in your own words what each value means
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Interprets the core values of the Constitution
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constitution and Daily Life
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the Constitution affects everyday life
steps: Identify three situations in daily life where rules or laws are needed
Explain how similar rules exist in the Constitution
Write a short explanation for each situation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects constitutional principles with real-life situations

activities_projects.title: Fundamental Rights Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of Fundamental Rights
steps: Choose one Fundamental Right
Describe a situation where this right could be violated
Explain how the Constitution protects people in that situation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies constitutional rights to real-life contexts

activities_projects.title: Comparing Rules and Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of rules in society
steps: List five rules followed in your school
Compare them with the rules of a country
Explain how both help maintain order
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the concept of governance through rules

activities_projects.title: Equality in Society
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning of equality in the Constitution
steps: Write two examples where people should be treated equally
Explain why discrimination is unfair
Relate your explanation to the Constitution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of equality as a constitutional value

activities_projects.title: Rights and Responsibilities Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between rights and duties
steps: List four rights that citizens have
Write the responsibilities that come with each right
Discuss why both are necessary in a democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the balance between rights and responsibilities

activities_projects.title: Mini Research on the Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the making of the Indian Constitution
steps: Find out when the Indian Constitution was adopted
Write the name of the leader who chaired the Drafting Committee
Explain why the Constitution is important for India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the historical background of the Constitution

activities_projects.title: Poster on Constitutional Values
steps: Choose one constitutional value such as liberty or justice
Create a poster explaining the value
Present your poster to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops visual representation of democratic values

activities_projects.title: Role Play: Following Rules
activities_projects.objective: Understand why rules are important in society
steps: Create a short role play about a situation without rules
Show what problems may occur
Explain how rules help solve the problem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the importance of laws and rules

activities_projects.title: Short Report on Constitutional Values
activities_projects.objective: Summarise the importance of the Constitution
steps: Write a short report about the role of the Constitution in India
Mention at least three values or principles
Explain why these values are important for citizens
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Summarises the role of the Constitution in democracy
